perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that it shall protect the confidentiality of, and take reasonable steps to prevent disclosure or unauthorized use of, the Confidential Information in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons not legally bound to maintain its confid entiality, provided that in no event shall such party's obligations exceed the reasonable standard of care taken to protect its own confidential information of like importance.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party is under no obligation to protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information. The parties are free to allow the Confidential Information to fall into the public domain.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the previously established obligation to protect confidential information. The statement creates an in-text contradiction because it releases both parties from any responsibility to maintain confidentiality, undermining the entire premise of the Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No copies of any Confidential Information ma y be made except to implement the purposes of this Agreement. Any materials, documents, notes, memoranda, software code, drawings, sketches and other tangible items containing, consisting of or relating to the Confidential Information of a party which are furnished hereunder to the other party, or are in the possession of the other party, remain the property of the party which disclosed the Confidential Information and shall be promptly returned to such party upon the disclosing party’s request therefore. Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting any rights, by license or otherwise, in any Confidential Information except as specified in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that parties are under no obligation to protect confidential information and can allow it to fall into the public domain. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which details restrictions on copying confidential information, states that it remains the property of the disclosing party, and limits rights to the information. The change introduces a direct and irreconcilable conflict, meeting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Any materials, documents, notes, memoranda, software code, drawings, sketches and other tangible items containing, consisting of or relating to the Confidential Information of a party which are furnished hereunder to the other party, or are in the possession of the other party, remain the property of the party which disclosed the Confidential Information and shall be promptly returned to such party upon the disclosing party’s request therefore.
perturbation.changed_text: Any materials, documents, notes, memoranda, software code, drawings, sketches and other tangible items containing, consisting of or relating to the Confidential Information of a party which are furnished hereunder to the other party, or are in the possession of the other party, become the sole property of the receiving party and need not be returned.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by stating that materials containing confidential information become the property of the receiving party, directly conflicting with the original clause which stipulates that these materials remain the property of the disclosing party and must be returned upon request.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose whatsoever except for the purposes set forth above. Each party agrees not to disclose the Confidential Information to any third person and only disclose the confidential information to its employees and those of its affiliates who have a need to know and who agree to keep such information confidential. Each party agrees that it shall protect the confidentiality of, and take reasonable steps to prevent disclosure or unauthorized use of, the Confidential Information in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons not legally bound to maintain its confid entiality, provided that in no event shall such party's obligations exceed the reasonable standard of care taken to protect its own confidential information of like importance. E ach party will promptly advise the other party in writing of any misappropriation or misuse by any person of such Confidential Information and provide assistance to the injured party in any legal proceedings related thereto. Each party acknowledges that its obligations hereunder survive in accordance with the terms hereof, notwithstanding the termination of the business relationship of the parties, for a period of two (2) years following the last disclo sure of Confidential Information by the other party hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The changed text states that materials containing confidential information become the sole property of the receiving party and need not be returned. The contradicted text states that each party agrees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose whatsoever except for the purposes set forth above. This creates a direct contradiction, as owning the information would imply the right to use it for any purpose, conflicting with the restriction on use specified in the contradicted text. Furthermore, the contradicted text implies the disclosing party retains ownership to some degree. Therefore, based on the given explanation, the changed text causes an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party's obligations under this Agreement shall not apply to information which: (a) is known by the receiving party or is publicly available at the time of disclosure; (b) becomes publicly available after disclosure by the disclosing party to the receiving party through no act of either party; (c) is hereafter rightfully furnished to the receiving party by a third party without restriction as to use or disclosure; (d) is disclosed with the prior written consent of the disclosing party; (e) is information that was independently developed by the receiving party; or (f) is required to be disclosed pursuant to any judicial or administrative proceeding, provided that the receiving party promptly notifies the disclosing party of such action and gives the disclosing party the opportunity to seek any legal remedies to maintain such information in confidence.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party's obligations under this Agreement shall not apply to information which: (a) is known by the receiving party or is publicly available at the time of disclosure; (b) becomes publicly available after disclosure by the disclosing party to the receiving party through the act of either party; (c) is hereafter rightfully furnished to the receiving party by a third party without restriction as to use or disclosure; (d) is disclosed with the prior written consent of the disclosing party; (e) is information that was independently developed by the receiving party; or (f) is required to be disclosed pursuant to any judicial or administrative proceeding, provided that the receiving party is not required to notify the disclosing party of such action and gives the disclosing party no opportunity to seek any legal remedies to maintain such information in confidence.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'through no act of either party' to 'through the act of either party' and removing the notification requirement in (f) creates an in-text contradiction. The original clause protected information that became public knowledge without either party's fault. Now, if either party causes the information to become public, it's no longer protected, conflicting with the original intent of the agreement and removing the opportunity to seek legal remedies, creating further uncertainty in the agreements enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose whatsoever except for the purposes set forth above. Each party agrees not to disclose the Confidential Information to any third person and only disclose the confidential information to its employees and those of its affiliates who have a need to know and who agree to keep such information confidential. Each party agrees that it shall protect the confidentiality of, and take reasonable steps to prevent disclosure or unauthorized use of, the Confidential Information in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons not legally bound to maintain its confid entiality, provided that in no event shall such party's obligations exceed the reasonable standard of care taken to protect its own confidential information of like importance. E ach party will promptly advise the other party in writing of any misappropriation or misuse by any person of such Confidential Information and provide assistance to the injured party in any legal proceedings related thereto. Each party acknowledges that its obligations hereunder survive in accordance with the terms hereof, notwithstanding the termination of the business relationship of the parties, for a period of two (2) years following the last disclo sure of Confidential Information by the other party hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states that a party's obligations do not apply if information becomes publicly available 'through no act of either party,' requiring notification and opportunity for legal remedies. The changed text removes this condition and the notification requirement, meaning that if a party *does* cause the information to become public, it's no longer protected, and no legal remedies can be pursued, directly conflicting with the contradicted text's requirement to protect confidentiality and prevent information from falling into the public domain. This falls under 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as different sections now provide conflicting obligations regarding the confidentiality of information.
